---
description: "GitHub Copilot code-review focus guidance — directs the code-review agent to high-value defects and away from harness-enforced mechanical concerns"
applyTo: '**'
excludeAgent: 'cloud-agent'
---

# Copilot Code-Review Focus

These instructions apply to **GitHub Copilot code review** for the `autoharness`
repository. The `excludeAgent: 'cloud-agent'` frontmatter keeps this file out of
GitHub's autonomous cloud coding agent, so review-only guidance never conflicts with
the repository-wide build and policy mandate in `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.

> **Applicability guard.** `excludeAgent` only distinguishes the cloud coding agent
> from code review; other surfaces (Copilot CLI, IDE Chat) may still load
> path-scoped instruction files. **If you are not performing GitHub Copilot code
> review, ignore the rest of this file** — none of the guidance below applies to
> writing code, running the pipeline, or any non-review task.

> **Activation note.** GitHub Copilot code review reads instruction files from the
> pull request's **base branch**, not the head branch. A change to this file takes
> effect for reviews only after it is merged into the base branch. The pull request
> that first installs this file is therefore validated structurally; its guidance
> governs subsequent pull requests.

## Focus on high-value concerns

Prioritize concrete, substantive defects that automated gates cannot reliably catch:

* Logic and correctness bugs
* Security vulnerabilities
* Secret or credential exposure
* Injection flaws (SQL, command, path, template)
* Missing or incorrect error handling
* Concurrency and race conditions
* API misuse and contract violations
* Data-loss or data-corruption risks
* High-blast-radius changes

Always report a concrete correctness, security, or data-loss defect, even when a
review persona, gate, or policy might also cover it. A finding in this list is never
a "nit."

## De-prioritize what the harness already enforces

This repository runs an automated agent harness that already enforces the concerns
below through deterministic gates, linters, and policies. De-prioritize ONLY these
purely mechanical, process, or style nits so review attention stays on substantive
defects:

* Markdown formatting and markdownlint heading hierarchy (MD001/MD025/MD041, P-008)
* Workflow policies P-001 through P-019 and constitutional principle sequencing
* Test-first red-green gate mechanics (P-002, P-004) and the `unittest` suite run
* Merge-commit-only discipline (P-009, Principle XI) — squash and rebase are forbidden
* Role-boundary enforcement (P-010) and single-worktree topology (P-016)
* Backlog and shipment bookkeeping conventions (backlogit)
* Template variable-completeness and cross-reference integrity checks

De-prioritizing is not suppression. Never suppress a substantive defect because it
resembles a mechanical concern. If a formatting, bookkeeping, or process issue
actually causes a correctness, security, or data-loss defect, report it as a defect.

## Review enforcement changes

When a change modifies a policy, gate, review persona, safety check, or CI
configuration, review it specifically for **weakened enforcement** — a removed gate,
a loosened threshold, a narrowed `applyTo` scope, a disabled check, or an exclusion
that quietly reduces protection. Flag any reduction in enforcement even when the
diff is otherwise small.
